title: Compound Tabs Component
description:
Build a **compound component** pattern for a Tabs component where `Tabs`, `TabList`, `Tab`, and `TabPanel` share state implicitly via React Context.

### Requirements

- `Tabs` is the root container; it manages the active tab index in state and provides it via context
- `TabList` renders its `Tab` children
- `Tab` receives its index implicitly (via `React.Children.map` or context); clicking it sets the active tab
- `TabPanel` receives its index implicitly; it only renders its children when its index matches the active index
- Active tab has a `data-active="true"` attribute and the class `tab--active`
- The component is fully controlled via the compound pattern — no explicit index props needed by the consumer
- Support a `defaultIndex` prop on `Tabs` (default 0)

### Usage Example

```tsx
<Tabs defaultIndex={0}>
  <TabList>
    <Tab>Tab 1</Tab>
    <Tab>Tab 2</Tab>
  </TabList>
  <TabPanel>Content 1</TabPanel>
  <TabPanel>Content 2</TabPanel>
</Tabs>
```

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Tab buttons | `tab-{index}` |
| Tab panels | `panel-{index}` |
constraints:
- Use the compound component pattern with React Context
- Tabs, TabList, Tab, TabPanel must be separate exported components
- Index assignment must be implicit (via React.Children.map or similar)
- Do not use render props or external state management
hints: Create a TabsContext that holds { activeIndex, setActiveIndex }. Provide it from the Tabs component.
In TabList, use React.Children.map to clone each Tab child with an implicit index prop.
In Tabs, also use React.Children.map on non-TabList children (TabPanels) to assign their indices.
